summary.less_than_5: Multimodal AI processes multiple types of input data (text, images, audio, video) simultaneously. It combines different modalities to understand context better. Think of it like how humans use multiple senses together.
summary.5_to_15: Multimodal models integrate vision, language, and audio processing through shared embeddings. They use attention mechanisms to align different modalities. Popular examples include GPT-4V and DALL-E 3.
summary.15_plus: Multimodal architectures employ transformer-based fusion strategies with cross-modal attention layers. They optimize joint embeddings in high-dimensional space while handling temporal and spatial alignment across modalities.

summary.less_than_5: AI Orchestration coordinates multiple AI models and services to work together. It manages workflow execution, data flow, and service integration. Like an orchestra conductor managing different instruments.
summary.5_to_15: Orchestration frameworks manage agent workflows, API calls, and state management. They handle error recovery, logging, and monitoring across distributed systems. Examples: LangChain, LangGraph.
summary.15_plus: Enterprise orchestration implements sophisticated routing policies, conditional execution flows, and dynamic resource allocation. It ensures ACID properties, handles cascading failures, and optimizes for throughput and latency.

summary.less_than_5: An AI Agent is an autonomous program that perceives its environment and takes actions. It uses reasoning to decide what to do next. Like a smart robot that plans its own actions.
summary.5_to_15: Agents combine LLMs with tools, memory, and reasoning capabilities. They can break down complex tasks into subtasks and interact with APIs. They use ReAct pattern: Reasoning → Action → Observation.
summary.15_plus: Advanced agents implement hierarchical planning, multi-step reasoning with tree-of-thought, and multi-turn interactions. They maintain context windows, manage memory efficiently, and optimize for cost and latency.

summary.less_than_5: Agent Swarm is a collection of AI agents working together to solve complex problems. Each agent has a role and collaborates with others. Like a team where each member has specific responsibilities.
summary.5_to_15: Swarms implement agent-to-agent communication, task delegation, and consensus mechanisms. They use protocols like FIPA-ACL for message passing. Examples: CrewAI, AutoGen.
summary.15_plus: Enterprise swarms implement Byzantine fault tolerance, dynamic topology changes, and emergent behavior patterns. They optimize for coordination overhead, implement sophisticated conflict resolution, and scale horizontally.

summary.less_than_5: AI Automation uses AI to automate repetitive business processes. It reduces manual work and improves efficiency. Examples: document processing, data entry, customer service.
summary.5_to_15: AI Automation combines RPA with ML for intelligent process automation. It learns from human actions and improves over time. Handles exception cases with AI decision-making.
summary.15_plus: Intelligent automation uses computer vision, NLP, and reinforcement learning for end-to-end process optimization. It includes continuous learning, anomaly detection, and predictive workflow optimization.

summary.less_than_5: Playwright is a framework for browser automation and testing. It controls browsers like Chrome, Firefox, and Safari. Used for web testing, scraping, and automation.
summary.5_to_15: Playwright provides cross-browser testing, parallel execution, and visual regression testing. It supports network interception, authentication handling, and waiting strategies for dynamic content.
summary.15_plus: Advanced Playwright usage includes CDP inspection, performance profiling, and custom plugins. It handles complex scenarios with multi-page navigation, service worker testing, and memory leak detection.

summary.less_than_5: RAG combines retrieval and generation to create accurate AI responses. It fetches relevant documents first, then generates answers based on them. Like checking references before writing.
summary.5_to_15: RAG systems use vector databases for semantic search, embedding models, and ranking algorithms. They improve LLM accuracy by grounding responses in real data. Examples: LlamaIndex, Langchain.
summary.15_plus: Advanced RAG includes hybrid retrieval, multi-hop reasoning, reranking strategies, and adaptive retrieval. It optimizes for latency, implements contextual chunking, and handles complex knowledge graphs.

summary.less_than_5: MCP is a protocol for AI models to access tools and resources. It standardizes how models interact with external services. Think of it as an API standard for AI.
summary.5_to_15: MCP defines client-server architecture for resource sharing. It includes type definitions, error handling, and capability negotiation. Enables interoperability across AI frameworks.
summary.15_plus: Enterprise MCP implementations include authentication, rate limiting, observability, and advanced routing. It supports federated deployments, semantic versioning, and backward compatibility.

summary.less_than_5: MCP Servers are applications that implement the MCP protocol. They expose tools and resources for AI models to use. Examples: database servers, API wrappers, file systems.
summary.5_to_15: MCP Servers handle resource management, error handling, and capability discovery. They implement request routing, session management, and capability negotiation.
summary.15_plus: Production MCP Servers include distributed deployment, caching strategies, load balancing, and observability. They handle complex authentication, implement circuit breakers, and optimize resource allocation.

summary.less_than_5: GAN pits two neural networks against each other: a generator and a discriminator. The generator creates fake data, the discriminator tries to detect fakes. They improve together.
summary.5_to_15: GANs use adversarial loss for training, implement spectral normalization, and use conditional information. Successful architectures: StyleGAN, Progressive GAN, CycleGAN.
summary.15_plus: Advanced GANs implement Wasserstein distance, gradient penalties, and multi-scale architectures. They optimize for mode collapse, implement self-attention, and use advanced sampling strategies.

summary.less_than_5: Generative AI creates new content: text, images, code, music. It learns patterns from data and generates new samples. Includes ChatGPT, DALL-E, Copilot.
summary.5_to_15: Generative models use transformers, diffusion, or auto-regressive architectures. They're trained on large datasets and fine-tuned for specific tasks. Support in-context learning.
summary.15_plus: Advanced generative AI includes efficient inference, prompt engineering optimization, alignment techniques, and multi-modal fusion. Implements retrieval augmentation and reinforcement learning from feedback.

summary.less_than_5: LLMs are large neural networks trained on vast text data. They predict the next word in a sequence. Examples: GPT-4, Claude, Llama. Used for chat, writing, coding.
summary.5_to_15: LLMs use transformer architecture with attention mechanisms. They're trained with next-token prediction and fine-tuned with RLHF. Support few-shot learning and in-context examples.
summary.15_plus: Advanced LLM topics include scaling laws, efficient transformers, mixture-of-experts, and advanced prompting strategies. Includes function calling, tool use, and constrained generation.

summary.less_than_5: VAE is a generative model that learns a compressed representation of data. It encodes data to latent space and decodes back. Used for generation and reconstruction.
summary.5_to_15: VAEs use probabilistic inference with KL divergence. They balance reconstruction loss and regularization. Good for semi-supervised learning and anomaly detection.
summary.15_plus: Advanced VAE techniques include beta-VAE for disentanglement, hierarchical VAEs, and conditional VAEs. Implements importance-weighted autoencoders and Wasserstein VAEs.

summary.less_than_5: Fine-tuning adapts a pre-trained model to a specific task. Start with weights from a large model, train on your data. Much faster than training from scratch.
summary.5_to_15: Fine-tuning strategies: frozen layers, learning rate scheduling, LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation). Handle domain shift and prevent catastrophic forgetting.
summary.15_plus: Advanced fine-tuning includes parameter-efficient methods (QLoRA, AdaLora), multi-task learning, and continual learning. Implements data selection strategies and curriculum learning.

summary.less_than_5: RL trains agents to make sequences of decisions to maximize rewards. Agent learns by interacting with environment. Like training through trial and error.
summary.5_to_15: RL uses value functions, policy gradients, and Q-learning. Important algorithms: DQN, A3C, PPO. Used in games, robotics, and optimization.
summary.15_plus: Advanced RL includes inverse reinforcement learning, hierarchical RL, and multi-agent RL. Implements curiosity-driven learning, meta-learning, and offline RL techniques.

references.title: RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback

summary.less_than_5: Supervised learning uses labeled data to train models. Input-output pairs teach the model. Used for classification (predict categories) and regression (predict values).
summary.5_to_15: Supervised learning includes decision trees, SVM, neural networks. Key concepts: train/test splits, cross-validation, hyperparameter tuning, overfitting.
summary.15_plus: Advanced supervised learning includes ensemble methods, meta-learning, and few-shot learning. Implements data augmentation strategies and advanced regularization techniques.

references.title: Understanding ML: From Theory to Practice

summary.less_than_5: Unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data. No correct answers provided. Includes clustering (grouping similar items) and dimensionality reduction.
summary.5_to_15: Algorithms: K-means, hierarchical clustering, DBSCAN, PCA, t-SNE. Used for data exploration, anomaly detection, and recommendation systems.
summary.15_plus: Advanced techniques: mixture models, manifold learning, and self-supervised learning. Implements variational inference and probabilistic graphical models.

summary.less_than_5: Diffusion models generate images by reversing noise. Start with pure noise, gradually denoise to create images. Powers DALL-E, Stable Diffusion, Midjourney.
summary.5_to_15: Models add noise to images gradually, then learn to reverse the process. Use Gaussian diffusion, implement score-based generative models. Conditional for text-to-image.
summary.15_plus: Advanced diffusion includes accelerated sampling, classifier-free guidance, and latent diffusion. Implements guidance scaling, inpainting, and control architectures like ControlNet.

references.title: Diffusion Models Beat GANs on Image Synthesis

summary.less_than_5: Transformer is the foundation of modern AI. Uses attention mechanism to process sequences in parallel. Replaced RNNs for most NLP tasks. Architecture: encoder-decoder.
summary.5_to_15: Key components: multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional encoding. Variants: BERT (encoder), GPT (decoder), T5 (encoder-decoder).
summary.15_plus: Advanced transformers: sparse attention, linear transformers, efficient attention mechanisms. Includes rotary embeddings, alibi positioning, and mixture-of-experts layers.

summary.less_than_5: NLP teaches computers to understand and generate human language. Includes text classification, sentiment analysis, translation, question answering.
summary.5_to_15: NLP tasks: tokenization, POS tagging, named entity recognition, parsing. Uses transformers, word embeddings (Word2Vec, GloVe), language models.
summary.15_plus: Advanced NLP: semantic role labeling, coreference resolution, knowledge extraction. Includes cross-lingual models, multilingual training, and structure-aware learning.

summary.less_than_5: Neural network is a model inspired by neurons in brains. Layers of interconnected nodes process information. Used for classification, regression, and complex pattern matching.
summary.5_to_15: Components: neurons, weights, biases, activation functions. Training: forward pass, loss calculation, backpropagation. Architectures: CNN, RNN, Transformer.
summary.15_plus: Advanced topics: optimization algorithms (Adam, AdamW), regularization (dropout, batch norm), architecture search, and neural architecture design patterns.

summary.less_than_5: Context window is the maximum length of text an LLM can process at once. GPT-4 has 128K tokens. Larger windows = more information the model sees.
summary.5_to_15: Context limitations: computation grows quadratically with length. Solutions: sparse attention, sliding windows, long-context training. Affects prompt design.
summary.15_plus: Advanced context techniques: ALiBi positional encoding, rotary embeddings, ring attention. Implements compression techniques and hierarchical retrieval for long documents.

summary.less_than_5: SLM are smaller language models optimized for efficiency. Good for edge devices, lower latency, lower cost. Examples: Phi, Gemma, Qwen.
summary.5_to_15: SLMs use knowledge distillation, pruning, and quantization. Train on curated data for high quality. Good for specific domains.
summary.15_plus: Advanced SLM techniques: mixture-of-experts scaling, efficient attention, and domain-specific optimization. Implements adaptive computation and early exit mechanisms.

summary.less_than_5: Ethical AI ensures AI systems are fair, transparent, and beneficial. Addresses bias, privacy, and accountability. Important for responsible deployment.
summary.5_to_15: Techniques: fairness metrics, bias detection, explainability methods. Includes privacy-preserving ML, differential privacy, and fairness-accuracy tradeoffs.
summary.15_plus: Advanced ethics: causality analysis, adversarial robustness, and governance frameworks. Implements regulatory compliance, audit trails, and impact assessment.

summary.less_than_5: LangChain is a framework for building LLM applications. Chains different components together: prompts, models, memory, tools. Simplifies LLM app development.
summary.5_to_15: LangChain features: chains, agents, retrievers, callbacks. Integrates with multiple LLM providers and vector databases. Handles prompt management and memory.
summary.15_plus: Advanced LangChain: custom tools, complex chains, streaming, and async processing. Implements observability, cost tracking, and advanced memory management.

summary.less_than_5: LangGraph is for building stateful, multi-actor AI applications. Models workflows as graphs with nodes and edges. Better control than simple chains.
summary.5_to_15: LangGraph features: state graphs, conditional edges, persistence, checkpointing. Handles complex multi-step workflows and human-in-the-loop patterns.
summary.15_plus: Advanced LangGraph: custom reducers, complex state management, and distributed execution. Implements cycle handling, version control, and advanced routing.

summary.less_than_5: CrewAI enables building teams of AI agents. Each agent has a role, goal, and tools. Agents collaborate to complete complex tasks.
summary.5_to_15: CrewAI features: agent roles, task definitions, tool integration, memory management. Handles agent-to-agent communication and task delegation.
summary.15_plus: Advanced CrewAI: custom processes, advanced memory, monitoring. Implements hierarchical task execution, performance optimization, and error recovery.

summary.less_than_5: AutoGen enables building multi-agent systems with conversable agents. Agents communicate to solve tasks. Supports code execution and tool use.
summary.5_to_15: AutoGen features: agent roles, conversation flows, code execution, tool integration. Handles nested conversations and reply chains.
summary.15_plus: Advanced AutoGen: custom agents, complex conversation patterns, and scaling. Implements distributed agents, monitoring, and advanced routing.

summary.less_than_5: Semantic Kernel by Microsoft enables building AI apps with plugins. Plugins are composable AI functions. Clean architecture for AI orchestration.
summary.5_to_15: Semantic Kernel features: skill definitions, memory, connectors, plugins. Integrates with multiple LLM providers and data sources.
summary.15_plus: Advanced Semantic Kernel: custom plugins, memory optimization, and distributed execution. Implements advanced scheduling and resource management.

summary.less_than_5: LlamaIndex (formerly GPT Index) is for building RAG systems. Indexes documents and retrieves relevant context for LLM queries.
summary.5_to_15: LlamaIndex features: document loading, indexing strategies, query engines, retrievers. Supports vector databases and hybrid search.
summary.15_plus: Advanced LlamaIndex: complex document hierarchies, re-ranking strategies, and multi-index querying. Implements adaptive retrieval and advanced chunking.

summary.less_than_5: Haystack is for building search and QA systems. Processes documents, builds search indices, and answers questions.
summary.5_to_15: Haystack features: pipelines, components, retrievers, readers. Integrates with multiple backends and LLM providers.
summary.15_plus: Advanced Haystack: complex pipelines, custom components, and advanced ranking. Implements distributed search and advanced analytics.

summary.less_than_5: DSPy is a programming model for LLM-based systems. Writes programs like normal code, then optimizes prompts automatically.
summary.5_to_15: DSPy features: signatures, modules, teleprompters (optimizers). Separates logic from prompts. Automatic optimization of prompts.
summary.15_plus: Advanced DSPy: custom modules, complex programs, and advanced optimization strategies. Implements meta-learning and adaptive prompt generation.

summary.less_than_5: PydanticAI builds AI agents with type safety. Uses Pydantic for data validation. Clean Python API for building agents.
summary.5_to_15: PydanticAI features: agent definition, tool integration, type validation. Handles streaming and structured output.
summary.15_plus: Advanced PydanticAI: custom tools, complex workflows, and optimized inference. Implements caching and performance monitoring.

summary.less_than_5: OpenAI Agents SDK provides official tools for building agents with GPT models. Includes agent functions, tool calling, and integration.
summary.5_to_15: Features: function calling, tool definitions, agent loop. Integrates seamlessly with OpenAI API and vision capabilities.
summary.15_plus: Advanced patterns: streaming agents, complex tool chains, and state management. Implements monitoring and cost optimization.

summary.less_than_5: Dify is a low-code platform for building LLM applications. Visual workflow builder, no coding required. Deploy agents and knowledge bases.
summary.5_to_15: Features: workflow builder, knowledge management, app publishing. Integrates with multiple LLM providers.
summary.15_plus: Advanced Dify: custom tools, complex workflows, and API deployment. Implements monitoring, versioning, and team collaboration.

summary.less_than_5: Flowise is a visual builder for LLM workflows. Drag-and-drop interface to build chatbots and workflows. No coding required.
summary.5_to_15: Features: node-based workflow builder, memory management, custom nodes. Integrates with multiple providers.
summary.15_plus: Advanced Flowise: custom node development, advanced state management, and scaling. Implements caching and performance optimization.
